item.title: Chinese Nationals Win Math’s Prestigious Fields Medal for First Time
item.description: The two U.S.-based mathematicians have become the first Chinese nationals to earn the honor considered math’s equivalent of the Nobel Prize.

item.title: China’s Campaign to Suffocate Taiwan Is Hitting Children’s Choirs and Baristas
item.description: Beijing has got creative in its continuing efforts to undermine the island’s international standing.

item.title: China’s Two-Speed Economy Pairs High-Tech Success With Domestic Gloom
item.description: The country’s soaring exports and tech prowess hide a brutal economic picture and a growing army of underemployed workers at home.

item.title: Inside China’s All-Out Push to Catch Up With American AI Chips
item.description: Xi confidant leads fevered effort for domestic alternatives to win AI race. Chinese AI companies get warned that anyone who resisted using local chips was a traitor.

item.title: China AI Companies Rush to Raise Funds and Close Gap With U.S.
item.description: Researchers and executives at Chinese AI companies say a shortage of investment and chips is holding them back in competition with leading American companies.

item.title: Washington and Beijing Are Sparring—and Still Making Plans
item.description: Plus, China’s Moonshot AI takes on U.S. rivals and how readers interpret the Chinese economy.

item.title: Trump Stirs China Tensions With Claims That It Meddled in U.S. Elections
item.description: The claims that China meddled in the 2020 elections threaten to erode an uneasy detente between the two superpowers.

item.title: As China Encroaches, Even New Zealand Is Getting Serious About Its Military
item.description: Billions will be spent in the coming years—including on missiles, drones and base upgrades—as the remote country squares up to a widening threat.

item.title: The U.S. Navy’s New Insurance Policy for War With China Is an Australian Base
item.description: The Pentagon plans to deploy submarines to an expanding facility in allies’ strategy to deter Beijing.
